'''Jeremy James Smith''' (born 18 July 1981) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]]. Primarily playing as a {{rlp|fe}} or {{rlp|hb}}, Smith has represented the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national]] teams.

==Playing career==Waiheke rams (NZ)is where it all started for Jeremy. He then moved to Australia and played for berala bears in the Canterbury bulldogs district, alongside future NRL players Matt utai, Junior Langi and Andrew Emelio. A [[St. George Dragons]] junior, Smith made the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 1998, 1999, and also 2000 but had pull out to play in the 2000 World Cup for NZ Māoris.<ref name=jnrkiwis>''New Zealand Rugby League Annual '98'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1998. p.181</ref> He played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|Aotearoa Māori]] side at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]]. In 2001 he played for the [[Hibiscus Coast Raiders]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]].<ref name=rd21>[http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=7743 Batercard Cup round 21 Preview] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20120907194322/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=7743 |date=2012-09-07}} ''rleague.com'', 10 August 2001</ref>

Smith played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] between 2005 and 2006.  Smith was suspended for four matches in 2006 after pushing a referee in Parramatta's bizarre 8–1 loss to [[St. George Illawarra|St. George]]. The judiciary had found that Smith pushed referee Sean Hampstead in the back with both hands after a scrum was ruled against Parramatta.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/league/smith-in-hot-water-after-pushing-referee-to-the-limit-on-wet-and-wild-night/2006/06/02/1148956548869.html|title = Smith in hot water after pushing referee to the limit on wet and wild night|date = 3 June 2006}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2006-06-06/parramatta-eels-smith-outed-for-referee-push/1771306|title=Parramatta Eels' Smith outed for referee push|newspaper=ABC News|date=6 June 2006|last1=Images|first1=Getty}}</ref> He has also represented the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Maori]] side.<ref name="League: Smith has big shoes to fill">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/section/4/story.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10468776|title=League: Smith has big shoes to fill|publisher=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |date=2007-10-09|accessdate=2008-08-02|first=Robert|last=Lowe}}</ref>

Smith then signed with South Sydney for the [[2007 NRL season]]. He penned his deal short after the announcement that his coach at the time, [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]] would also join the South Sydney club in 2007.  Smith joined the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] at the beginning of the 2009 season.  Smith again played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] in 2010 against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_10681035">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10681035&ref=rss|title=League: Maori draw with England|date=16 October 2010|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|accessdate=6 October 2011}}</ref>

Smith has signed a one-year deal with [[Super League]] outfit, [[Wakefield Trinity]] ahead of the 2011 season. Smith was injured during the off season and could not play in any friendlies due to visa problems, which meant he had little match fitness when he made his début in Round 3 against Salford.  He returned to Australia in 2012, signing with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and playing [[NSW Cup]] for the [[Illawarra Cutters]].<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=43777 NSW Cup Rd 12 Illawarra 17 Newtown 32] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130201174531/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=43777 |date=2013-02-01}} ''rleague.com'', 23 May 2012</ref>
